posts.text: I've been reading conflicting studies lately. Some parents worry about long-term effects. The MMR vaccine schedule seems aggressive to them. I am trying to understand the actual risk tradeoffs. Thanks for any pointers.

posts.text: Welcome back to the channel. Today we have a long discussion lined up. First we cover recent policy news. Then we dig into historical outbreaks. After that we summarize what changed in the last decade. Many viewers asked about childhood immunization trends. Public health officials emphasize routine shots. Some communities still see lower uptake than targets. In the middle segment we compare notes with a pediatric nurse. She explains how clinics handle vaccine hesitancy. We also touch on school requirements in several states. Later we discuss travel and import risks. Measles cases abroad can affect local clusters. Clinics watch for fever and rash presentations. We wrap with resources for families. Thanks for watching and see you next week.
